title: The Emperor of All Maladies: A Biography of Cancer
originalTitle: The Emperor of All Maladies: A Biography of Cancer
author: Siddhartha Mukherjee
keyConcepts: Cancer treated as an evolving biography rather than a static disease
Surgery, radiation, and chemotherapy as distinct therapeutic epochs
The physical and psychological limits of surgery on cancer
Radiation as both cure and carcinogen
Chemotherapy discovered serendipitously from chemical warfare agents
The War on Cancer (Nixon, 1971) as bureaucratic and institutional transformation
Genetics and the molecular revolution in oncology
Targeted therapy and the molecular-profit model (Gleevec as archetype)
The role of tumour suppressor genes, especially p53
Immunotherapy as the third revolution in cancer therapy
Cancer survivorship and prevention as unfinished chapters
The physician's emotional experience of treating cancer
The future of cancer: liquid biopsies, personalised medicine, immunotherapy
awards: PEN/E. O. Wilson Literary Science Writing Award (2011)

documentaryAdaptation.title: Cancer: The Emperor of All Maladies
